name: Chard and Parsley Quiche with Two Cheese
recipeIngredient: plus extra for working the dough
1/3 cup BelPaese or similar mild, soft cheese
recipeInstructions:
To make the pastry shell:
1. Sift together the flour and salt into a bowl.
2. Cut the chilled butter and margarine into 1/2-inch chunks and add them to the flour mixture.
3. Using a pastry blender or 2 knives, cut in the butter and margarine until the mixture forms pea-size balls.
4. Add the ice water, 1 tablespoon at a time, while turning the dough with a fork and then with your fingertips just enough to dampen it.
5. Gather the dough into a ball, wrap it in aluminum foil or plastic wrap and refrigerate for 15 minutes.
To prepare the crust:
6. Preheat the oven to 475 degrees.
7. On a lightly floured work surface, roll out the chilled dough into a round about 11 1/2 inches in diameter and 1/8 inch thick.
8. Drape the pastry over the rolling pin and carefully transfer it to a 9-to-10-inch tart pan.
9. Unfold it from the pin and press it gently into the pan, trimming off the overlap even with the pan's rim.
10. Line the tart shell with aluminum foil and then add a layer of pastry weights or dried beans.
11. Place the weighted pastry pan on a baking sheet and place both in the preheated oven to bake partially until faintly golden, 8 or 9 minutes.
To make the filling:
12. Remove from the oven, lift out the weights, remove the aluminum foil, and let the crust cool completely on the baking sheet.
13. Reduce the oven heat to 375 degrees.
14. Arrange the chard leaves on a steamer rack over gently boiling water, cover and steam for 5 to 7 minutes until the leaves retain their pretty green color but are greatly reduced in volume.
15. Remove from the steamer and squeeze the leaves to extract any water.
16. Cut the chard into chiffonade strips by stacking the leaves, rolling them up into a thin cigar shape, and then cutting them crosswise in thin shreds. Set aside.
17. Steam the parsley leaves for 3 to 5 minutes, remove from the steamer, squeeze dry, and mince the parsley.
18. Squeeze the chard and parsley to remove excess water.
19. Warm the olive oil in a skillet over low heat, add the garlic and saute until just soft, about 1 or 2 minutes.
20. Add the chard and parsley greens, reduce the heat to very low and cook, turning often, until the greens have absorbed the oil, about 5 minutes.
21. Remove from the heat and set aside.
22. In a large mixing bowl, combine the cream, salt, pepper, eggs, olive oil-infused greens, and cheeses.
23. Fill the partially cooked shell with the mixture without spilling any over the top, cutting the butter into bits and dotting the top of the quiche.
24. Place in the preheated 375-degree oven and bake until the filling is puffed and golden and a knife inserted in the center comes out clean, about 30 to 35 minutes.
To serve:
25. Remove from the oven and serve immediately or let cool to room temperature.
26. Cut into wedges to serve.
